key_insight: A hybrid-dimensional Biot solver combines XFEM, Lagrange multipliers, and optimization-based domain decomposition to handle coupled flow and mechanics in fractured porous media with nonconforming meshes.
engineering_relevance: This approach enables robust simulation of fracture-induced pressure and displacement discontinuities in complex 3D porous media, which is important for geomechanics, reservoir engineering, and subsurface flow modeling.
method: XFEM on the 3D mesh allows fractures to arbitrarily cut elements while representing discontinuous displacement and pressure fields.
Mechanical equilibrium is posed as a saddle-point problem with Lagrange multipliers enforcing displacement continuity across fractures.
A fixed-stress splitting scheme decouples flow and mechanics, and the mixed-dimensional pressure subproblem is solved with conjugate gradient iterations.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports that the fixed-stress splitting combined with the CG solver is highly effective for this mixed-dimensional fractured-media problem class.
watch_item: Performance may depend on the quality of the XFEM discretization and solver setup, especially for strongly coupled or highly complex fracture networks.
paper_title: Hybrid-Dimensional Biot Problem with an Optimization Based Domain Decomposition Approach
